items.title: Reality of Commercial AI World from a 16 Year Old's Perspective
items.why_it_matters: A 16-year-old developer shared their raw experience building AI voice agents and open-source tools, explaining why cold outreach failed but building real products worked. It is a highly relatable reality check on what it actually takes to build and launch things independently at your age. This week, you can look at his open-source OpenClaw clone on GitHub to see how he structured his local agent execution code.

items.title: Show HN: A prompt bar that lets my website edit itself (self-hosted Claude Code)
items.why_it_matters: A developer built a self-hosted prompt bar that allows your website to edit its own code using Claude Code. Since you are building AI-ingest with Next.js, this is a highly practical way to experiment with self-editing UI. This week, you can clone his repository and integrate the prompt bar into a test branch of your Next.js project to let Claude edit your UI components directly from the browser.

items.title: Show HN: You can continue a Claude session in Codex, and vice versa
items.why_it_matters: A new utility called `agent-convert` lets you seamlessly hand off and continue active Claude sessions inside Codex, and vice versa. Since you use both Claude Code and Codex daily, this removes the friction of losing context when switching between your terminal and your editor. This week, you can install the CLI tool and use it to transition your current AI-ingest coding session from your terminal directly into Codex.

items.title: Show HN: Reverse-engineering web apps into agent tools
items.why_it_matters: This tool runs inside an authenticated web app, monitors its API calls, and automatically converts them into tools/MCP servers that your AI agent can use. This makes it incredibly easy to connect Claude Code to external services without writing complex API integrations from scratch. This week, you can run this tool on your Supabase dashboard to generate a custom MCP tool that lets Claude Code manage your database schema directly.

items.title: Ask HN: I hate coding agents. Is this skill issue?
items.why_it_matters: A viral Hacker News thread discusses the mental fatigue and loss of code ownership that comes with relying too heavily on coding agents. For a vibe coder like you, this is a crucial warning on how to maintain control of your codebase as you direct the AI. This week, read the top comments to learn their strategies, and commit to writing a manual unit test for one Supabase query in your project before letting Claude write the code for it.

items.title: Claude reset 5-hour and weekly rate limits for all users
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has reset the 5-hour and weekly rate limits for all Claude users, giving you a clean slate of high-tier usage. This means you have fewer interruptions and can run much longer development sessions without hitting a wall. This week, block out a 3-hour deep-work session to let Claude Code refactor your AI-ingest backend without worrying about hitting your rate limits.

items.title: Close the laptop and Claude keeps going
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic announced that Claude can now continue executing long-running tasks even after you close your laptop. This is perfect for complex refactoring or multi-file code generation tasks that usually force you to sit and wait at your terminal. This week, trigger a large-scale code cleanup or test-generation command in Claude Code, close your laptop, and let it run in the background while you take a break.

items.title: Show HN: Policy enforcement for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex
items.why_it_matters: Kastra is a new runtime authorization tool that intercepts tool calls from Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor, evaluating them against policies before they run to prevent accidental destructive commands. As you use AI to write code and interact with your database, this adds a safety net against accidental data loss. This week, you can install Kastra locally and set up a simple policy that blocks Claude Code from running any destructive SQL commands on your Supabase database.
